metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the `process_snapshot` guard rationale: `claimed_session` is already seeded by `make_initial_state()` so no active guard is needed in `process_snapshot` — either remove it from scope (the simpler, correct approach) or, if kept for belt-and-suspenders consistency, update the rationale to say it is redundant-but-explicit (not 'instead of relying solely on make_initial_state').
findings.finding: The plan's `process_snapshot` defensive `setdefault('claimed_session', None)` guard is stated to be needed so 'a pre-feature snapshot yields an explicit None (key-present) instead of relying solely on make_initial_state', but `make_initial_state()` unconditionally seeds `claimed_session: None` before any event replay, and the restore loop only overwrites keys that ARE present in `compiled_state` — so a pre-feature snapshot already leaves `state['claimed_session']` at `None` without the guard. Unlike `managed_refs` (which requires active seeding via `seed_managed_refs_from_current`) or `attestations` (which requires active folding), `claimed_session` needs no active guard in `process_snapshot`. The plan's positive rationale for the guard is inaccurate, and an implementer adding the guard 'matching the managed_refs / attestations pattern' will add dead code with a misleading comment.
findings.suggested_fix: Drop the `process_snapshot` setdefault guard from scope for `claimed_session` (unlike `managed_refs`/`attestations`, no active restoration computation is required). The compaction AC can still be tested by asserting that pre-feature snapshot replay yields `state['claimed_session'] is None`, which passes via `make_initial_state()` alone.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten the AC5 doc-content assertion to verify that `claimed_session`, `data["session"]`, and `compaction` co-appear within the same section block (e.g. assert all three strings are found within N lines of each other, or within the same `##` heading, rather than anywhere in the file).
findings.finding: The doc-content AC (AC5) uses a plain substring presence check ('asserts the section names `claimed_session`, `data["session"]`, and "compaction"') without anchoring. While the strings are distinctive, this is a verify-command defect type-1 (substring false-positive) risk: the assertion could pass if 'compaction' appears only in an unrelated cross-reference sentence rather than in the dedicated `claimed_session` documentation section the AC intends to prove.
findings.suggested_fix: In the test, locate the `## claimed_session` heading offset, then assert all three target strings appear within that section's text slice: `section = doc[doc.index('## claimed_session'):]; assert 'data["session"]' in section and 'compaction' in section`.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `csn` (the `to_llm` compact alias for `claimed_session`) to the `docs/event-schema.md` LLM-format documentation and extend the AC5 doc-content assertion to verify its presence.
findings.finding: The plan adds `"claimed_session": "csn"` to `KEY_MAP` in `llm_format.py`, introducing a new compact-key alias `csn` in the LLM read-surface contract, but the `docs/event-schema.md` section described in the plan (and its AC doc-content test) only asserts presence of `claimed_session`, `data["session"]`, and "compaction" — the `csn` alias is never mentioned in any documentation target.
findings.suggested_fix: In the `docs/event-schema.md` `claimed_session` section, add a sentence such as: 'In the compact LLM read format (`to_llm`), `claimed_session` is rendered under the key `csn`; the value is omitted when `None`.' Then add `"csn"` to the doc-content test assertion list alongside `claimed_session` and `compaction`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `ticket_state_llm.schema.json` to the edit set: update its `description` key-map list to include `claimed_session→csn` and add a `csn` entry to `properties` (type `["string","null"]`, description `claimed_session`).
findings.finding: `src/rebar/schemas/ticket_state_llm.schema.json` is an unenumerated consumer: adding `claimed_session → csn` to `KEY_MAP` causes `--output llm` to emit the `csn` key for claimed tickets, but the plan names neither a `ticket_state_llm.schema.json` update (description key-map list + properties entry) nor a companion AC asserting the LLM schema documents `csn`.
findings.suggested_fix: In `src/rebar/schemas/ticket_state_llm.schema.json`, append `claimed_session→csn` to the description's key map sentence, and add `"csn": { "type": ["string", "null"], "description": "claimed_session" }` to the `properties` object.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `csn` as a named property in `src/rebar/schemas/ticket_state_llm.schema.json` (type `['string','null']`, description `'claimed_session — coding-agent session id set on the open→in_progress claim edge'`) and update the schema's `description` key-map string to include `claimed_session→csn`, keeping the LLM schema in sync with KEY_MAP.
findings.finding: The plan adds `'claimed_session': 'csn'` to `KEY_MAP` in `llm_format.py` but does not mention updating `ticket_state_llm.schema.json` to enumerate the `csn` property. Every other KEY_MAP entry (`asn`, `pid`, `sig`, `att`, etc.) is documented as a named property in that schema. The schema's `description` string also enumerates the key map inline. The plan's stated scope for the LLM read format change is incomplete — `ticket_state_llm.schema.json` is the validation oracle for `--output llm` and the drift between the live compact key and the schema will persist silently (the schema's `additionalProperties: true` prevents `test_ticket_state_llm` from catching the gap).
findings.suggested_fix: Add to `ticket_state_llm.schema.json` properties: `"csn": { "type": ["string", "null"], "description": "claimed_session" }` and append `claimed_session→csn` to the description's key map list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `csn` (with description `claimed_session`) to `src/rebar/schemas/ticket_state_llm.schema.json`'s `properties` block and update the schema's `description` key-map string to include `claimed_session→csn`.
findings.finding: The plan adds `"claimed_session": "csn"` to `KEY_MAP` in `llm_format.py`, introducing a new compact-key alias `csn` into the LLM output contract, but the plan never calls out updating `src/rebar/schemas/ticket_state_llm.schema.json` to enumerate this alias. That schema is the authoritative published vocabulary for `to_llm` output (its `description` string lists all current compact keys; its `properties` block enumerates them). The `csn` alias will be silently absent from the schema any LLM consumer or schema-pinning test relies on.
findings.suggested_fix: In `ticket_state_llm.schema.json`, add `"csn": { "type": ["string", "null"], "description": "claimed_session" }` to `properties`, and append `claimed_session→csn` to the `description` key-map sentence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `claimed_session → csn` to the `Key mapping:` docstring table in `src/rebar/reducer/llm_format.py`.
findings.finding: The plan adds `"claimed_session": "csn"` to the `KEY_MAP` dict but does not call out updating the hand-maintained `Key mapping:` docstring table at the top of `llm_format.py` (lines 9–24). The docstring is the co-located human-readable contract for the key abbreviations; leaving it stale means the processing protocol (the compact key) is defined in the dict but absent from the co-located documentation block that LLM tooling and maintainers read.
findings.suggested_fix: Append `  claimed_session → csn` to the key-mapping comment block at the top of `llm_format.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC5 to also assert that `docs/event-schema.md` (or `ticket_state_llm.schema.json`) documents the `csn` compact alias for `claimed_session`.
findings.finding: AC5 (doc-content test) asserts that `docs/event-schema.md` contains the strings `claimed_session`, `data["session"]`, and `"compaction"`, but does NOT require the compact alias `csn` to appear. The plan's What section says `docs/event-schema.md` should document the field; yet the LLM-format compact key `csn` — which is the token an LLM agent actually encounters when reading `to_llm` output — is never required by any AC or doc-content check to be present in any doc, leaving the compact-key vocabulary undocumented on all authoritative read surfaces.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the RETIRE path for `claimed_session`: specify which event (e.g., an UNCLAIM/STATUS with no session) triggers clearing it to `None` in the fold, or explicitly document that it is intentionally write-once and note the implication for session expiry.
findings.finding: No RETIRE/cleanup lifecycle path is defined for `claimed_session`: the plan specifies CREATE (S2), CONSUME (show/MCP/to_llm), and a compaction guard, but never defines what event or fold path clears `claimed_session` back to `None` when a session ends or a ticket is released/unclaimed. A stale session id will persist indefinitely in `compiled_state`.
findings.location: ## What — Compaction safety / _processors.py; ## Scope; ## Depends on
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the What/Scope sections: 'Clearing `claimed_session` on session release is handled by [event X] in the fold (S2 scope) — if not yet landed, add a follow-up AC or note that the field is write-once-per-claim-cycle with no automatic expiry in this story.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
